Photograph by Marli Bryant Miller Written by Martin Richard This huge rock is in the Willamette Valley in Oregon. It is different from all the other rocks around it. How did it get there? Where did it come from? Here is one clue: it has parallel scratch marks on it! Those scratch marks are found on rocks that have been dragged by glaciers. (We wrote about that right here.) So we are very sure that this rock was scratched when it was dragged by a glacier. What else do we know about this rock? Scientists have taken pieces of this rock and analyzed the minerals. The minerals are the same as rocks from northwest Montana. That's over 500 miles away! So we have good evidence that this rock came from northwest Montana and that a glacier dragged it around. Were there glaciers in northwest Montana? Yes, at the end of the last ice age, northwestern Montana was partially covered by glaciers. But this rock is in the Willamette Valley of Oregon! The glacier was not 500 miles long. But there is a way that a piece of a glacier could have gotten to Oregon. It could have floated there, as an iceberg! Floated on what? There is no ocean between Montana and Oregon. There is a river though. Well, not one river, but a system of rivers, which flow from Montana all the way to the ocean. Those rivers flow into the Columbia River, and this rock is near the Willamette River which flows into the Columbia. Could an iceberg from a Montana glacier have made it all the way to Oregon? There is a lot off evidence that there was a huge lake in northwestern Montana at the end of the last ice age. A huge glacier from Canada made an ice dam on the Clark Fork River. The lake that formed behind the dam is called Glacial Lake Missoula. It was more than a thousand feet deep at the dam, and it was huge. Glacial Lake Missoula covered almost 3,000 square miles, and held more water than Lake Erie and Lake Ontario combined! (Actually, the glacier dammed the river several times. But the first lake that formed seems to have been the deepest and the biggest, and we are going to talk about that one.) When that first ice dam broke, all that water behind it burst out in a huge wall. In some places, the wall of water was more than 500 feet high!! There is evidence that the water moved through the narrow valleys at almost 60 miles an hour! We can tell how deep the water was, because it stripped off the soil of the valley walls, stripped it right down to the rock. Multiply the height of the valley by the width and multiply that by the speed of the flow and you get the volume of flow per hour: 9 cubic MILES of water per hour. Let's try to imagine 9 cubic miles of water. The next time you go outside, look to the east, where the sun comes up. Look at something you think is two miles away, and imagine a line going there. Now look to your right, to the south. Look at something in that direction that is two miles away. Imagine that line. Those two lines make a square two miles on a side. It has an area of 2 x 2 = 4 square miles. Now look straight up and try to imagine a line two miles high. You are now standing at the corner of an imaginary cube, 2 miles on each side. Your cube has a volume of 2 x 2 x 2 = 8 cubic miles. Now fill that cube with water. Make the sides just a bit longer, to just under two and a tenth miles (2.08, actually), and you have 9 cubic miles of water. That's how much water flowed at the peak of the flood from Glacial Lake Missoula. In an hour! How does that compare to other rivers? The Amazon has the largest flow of any river in the world: about one hundredth of a cubic mile per hour. So the flow of the Glacial Lake Missoula Flood was 900 times bigger! Try to imagine 900 Amazons in one flood! A cube of water one yard on each side weighs just about 1,700 pounds, just under a ton. Imagine the weight of YOUR cube of water, two MILES on each side. Imagine the force and the power of that water as it raged through the canyons at 60 miles an hour, with giant whirlpools breaking and stripping the rock itself. Imagine the roar of the water, the crashing of great boulders slamming into each other and into the valley walls. That would make a great movie! But let's get back to our rock. The rock has parallel scratch marks. That tells us it was once in a glacier. The minerals of the rock tell us it came from northwest Montana. Other evidence tells us there was once a huge lake in behind an ice dam in northern Montana. That lake had glaciers on its shores, which means there were icebergs on the lake. When the ice dam broke, it unleashed a gigantic, humongous, terrifying, titanic, stupendous, colossal, awesome monster of a flood, which ripped apart and re-arranged the land as it raged to the sea, carrying icebergs which carried rocks and carried this rock 500 miles. What a trip this rock has taken, what a tale this rock could tell! Oh wait. It IS telling us. All we have to do is pay attention, collect the facts, and imagine the story that explains the facts. When we do that, this rock tells us a great story: of the humungous floods from Glacial Lake Missoula. You want to learn more about the story of Glacial Lake Missoula and the Humungous Flood? You could start here, and explore the links. [starts to get down from the chair] I see a white dog looking at me. I mean I did this one just 'cause it was so tight. Title: How to Perform a Two-Foot Spin on Ice Performing a two-foot spin on ice is an essential skill for figure skaters looking to improve their balance, agility, and overall technique. This move requires practice and patience, but with determination and consistency, you can master this fundamental jump. Here's our comprehensive guide on executing a perfect two-foot spin on ice. ### Step 1: Understand the Move Before attempting any new skating maneuver, understanding its mechanics and purpose is crucial. A two-foot spin involves rotating your body while maintaining balance on both feet. It serves as the foundation for more advanced spins such as sit spins, camel spins, and layback spins. By learning to control your center of gravity over two feet, you will develop better stability, posture, and edge awareness. ### Step 2: Prepare Your Skates Ensure that your skates are properly sharpened and fitted before practicing spins. Properly sharpened blades provide adequate grip during turns and edges required for successful spinning techniques. Consult with a professional if necessary. ### Step 3: Practice Balancing Stand upright at the center of the rink or a clear area where there aren't many distractions. Position yourself so that one foot faces forward (toe pick facing outward) and the other faces backward (heel pointing outwards). Both heels should be touching each other forming a 'T'. Keep your knees slightly bent and engage your core muscles. Hold this position until comfortable and confident enough to proceed. Key Tip: Maintain equal weight distribution between both legs. Avoid leaning towards either side. ### Step 4: Initiate the Turn Slowly begin turning by pushing off gently with the inside edge of your back foot. Use your arms for momentum; extend them away from your body then bring them close together as you turn. Continue turning until you return to your original starting point. Remember to keep your knees bent and maintain good posture throughout the process. Guideline: Focus on keeping your head up and eyes level. Looking down may disrupt your balance and alignment. ### Step 5: Add More Rotation Once you feel stable performing single rotations, challenge yourself by adding another half rotation after completing the first one. To do this, push harder with the inside edge of your back foot when initiating the second turn. Gradually increase the number of rotations as you gain confidence and proficiency. Key Tip: Control speed with precision. Don't rush into multiple rotations without solidifying your foundational skills. ### Step 6: Refine Your Technique As you progress, pay attention to details like keeping your shoulders aligned, chin parallel to the ice, and ensuring even pressure on both skate blades. Smooth transitions between turns enhance fluidity and gracefulness. Additionally, remember to exhale smoothly rather than holding your breath which could cause tension buildup leading to loss of balance. ### Step 7: Consistent Practice Like all physical activities, regular practice reinforces muscle memory and enhances performance. Set aside dedicated time daily or weekly to work on your two-foot spin. Over time, what initially felt challenging becomes effortless. Remember always to prioritize safety. If feeling unsteady, stop immediately and regroup. Falling incorrectly might lead to injuries. With consistent efforts, you'll soon execute flawless two-foot spins! This guide will provide you with detailed steps and useful tips to help you get her attention and spark her interest effectively. The following methods emphasize respectful, genuine engagement rather than manipulative tactics. Remember that building meaningful connections takes time; stay patient and persistent without being pushy or disrespectful. Step 1: Show Genuine Interest in Her as an Individual Explanation: Demonstrating authentic curiosity about her thoughts, feelings, experiences, and aspirations is crucial for gaining her attention and interest. People naturally respond positively when they feel seen, heard, and valued by others. Key Tips: * Ask open-ended questions to encourage her to share more about herself. Examples include: "What excites you most these days?" or "Can you tell me more about your passion for painting?" * Listen actively during conversations. Maintain eye contact, nod occasionally, and paraphrase what she says to show understanding (e.g., "So, you're saying..."). * Engage with her online content (if applicable), such as social media posts or blogs. Well it's really a boy dressed up. See if we can open for real if it's ready. The relative pronouns are: We use who and whom for people, and which for things. Or we can use that for people or things. We use relative pronouns: after a noun, to make it clear which person or thing we are talking about: the house that Jack built the woman who discovered radium an eight-year-old boy who attempted to rob a sweet shop in relative clauses to tell us more about a person or thing: My mother, who was born overseas, has always been a great traveller. Lord Thompson, who is 76, has just retired. We had fish and chips, which is my favourite meal. But we do not use that as a subject in relative clauses. We use whose as the possessive form of who: This is George, whose brother went to school with me. We sometimes use whom as the object of a verb or preposition: This is George, whom you met at our house last year. This is George's brother, with whom I went to school. But nowadays we normally use who: This is George, who you met at our house last year. This is George's brother, who I went to school with. When whom or which have a preposition the preposition can come at the beginning of the clause... I had an uncle in Germany, from who[m] I inherited a bit of money. We bought a chainsaw, with which we cut up all the wood. or at the end of the clause: I had an uncle in Germany who[m] I inherited a bit of money from. We bought a chainsaw, which we cut all the wood up with. We can use that at the beginning of the clause: I had an uncle in Germany, that I inherited a bit of money from. We bought a chainsaw, that we cut all the wood up with. - Determiners and quantifiers - Clause, phrase and sentence Tags for teachers A - Z of Content - 1 de 6 I'm going to give him some mashed potatoes. [mother points to Nina's large stuffed rabbit in the corner of her room.] Using Articles: A, An, The (printable version here) Rules for using articles often confuse students who learn English as their second language. Even native speakers of English can have problems in this area! Here are a few pointers: "A" is used before singular words beginning with a consonant or a word beginning in "h" where the letter is pronounced; "an" is used before singular words beginning in a vowel or with a "silent h": "An eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth." "A historian gave us a hand for an hour when we were planting an heirloom rose bush in a hothouse. It was an honorable thing to do, so we sang a hymn of praise." "A" or "an" precede words that refer to general things; "the" comes before words referring to specific things. Imagine two policemen having a conversation: "Are you sure this is the man who stole the car?" "No. A witness saw a man, but the man we arrested is not the one." "Well, he confessed to stealing a car." "That's true, but was it the car we picked up on Plum Street?" Sometimes these straightforward rules can get confusing. Consider these examples: "That is a book you borrowed last month." "That is the book you borrowed last month." Note that both are correct! In the first example, the second speaker implies that the other person may have borrowed more than one book last month. In the second example, there is no doubt: last month the first person borrowed one book from the second person. Some general words take no article at all. - Plural words: - "Maybe he steals cars for a living." - "Maybe he wears disguises; thieves can do that." - Words signifying things that cannot be counted easily. Words such as "sand, milk, fruit" and other things that are measured in quantity can be preceded by a measurement (eight cans, a gallon, a pound): - "We found sand in the car." ("the sand" if it was some specific sand that was being discussed). "Where was the sand? In the trunk or on the carpet? Did you find anything else?" "We found beer in the trunk." "How much beer?" "Eight cans of beer." - Words signifying abstract concepts, ideas, or subjects: - "I'm studying biology this year." - "What are you doing in philosophy?" "We're studying the history of knowledge." (a specific type of history, but not "the knowledge"--knowledge is a general concept). Estimating your Uber fare is an easy process that can help you budget for your transportation needs and avoid any unexpected costs. By following these steps, you'll be able to accurately estimate the cost of your next Uber ride: 1. **Open the Uber app:** The first step to estimating your Uber fare is to open the Uber app on your smartphone. If you don't have the app yet, download it from the App Store (iOS) or Google Play Store (Android). Once you have the app installed, log in with your account information or sign up if you haven't already done so. 2. **Enter your destination:** Tap the "Where to?" box at the bottom of the screen. Type in the address of your intended location or allow Uber to access your current location by enabling Location Services. This will automatically fill in your pickup point. Make sure to double-check all addresses before proceeding. 3. **Choose your vehicle type:** Based on availability and demand, several types of vehicles may appear as options. These include UberX, UberPOOL, UberBLACK, UberSUV, and others depending on your region. Each option offers different levels of comfort, capacity, and pricing. For example, UberPOOL allows multiple riders to share a trip, which often results in lower fares compared to other options. However, this might take longer due to additional stops along the route. Select the most suitable vehicle based on your preferences and requirements. 4. **Review estimated fare:** After selecting your destination and vehicle type, tap "Confirm" to view the estimated fare for your trip. Note that this is just an approximation; actual prices may vary due to factors such as traffic conditions, road construction, and surge pricing during peak hours. Keep in mind that surcharges like tolls, taxes, and booking fees are also included in the final price. 5. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * To get more accurate estimates, make sure your GPS is enabled, and you permit the app to access your device's location services. * Be aware that surge pricing occurs when there's high demand for rides in a specific area, causing prices to increase temporarily. You'll see a multiplier applied to the base rate when checking your estimated fare during times of surge pricing. * Consider using Uber's Split Fare feature if you're traveling with friends. It evenly divides the total cost among all passengers who opt into splitting the fare, making it easier to split expenses without having to calculate individual shares manually. * Always check for available promotions or discount codes within the app prior to confirming your ride. Sometimes, Uber offers special deals or incentives that can save you money on your journey. * Remember to factor in tipping etiquette when considering the overall cost of your Uber ride. While optional, many people choose to add a small gratuity for their driver. Look it FAE it's a XXX monster. Well, why do want us to clean it up? And their mother asked where they wanted to eat. As a copyright license, the GFDL is a type of contract between the creator of a copyrightable work (such as a book, an encyclopedia article, a painting, or a piece of music) and anyone else who might want to use it. The GFDL is considered "copyleft" because the license is meant to make it easier to use and re-use the copyrighted work, not to restrict its use. Look make it come right in the little house. Gotta peek under there to put his pants on. Besides the force of gravity and the electromagnetic forces, there are weak and strong nuclear forces. "Weak nuclear forces" are the forces that cause radioactivity, or when matter emits energy. Weak nuclear force works on particles with a spin of 1/2. "Strong nuclear forces" are the forces that keep the quarks in a neutron and a proton together, and keeps the protons and neutrons together in an atom. The particle that carries the strong nuclear force is thought to be a gluon. The gluon is a particle with a spin of 1. The gluon holds together quarks to form protons and neutrons. However, the gluon only holds together quarks that are three different colors. This makes the end product have no color. This is called "confinement". Creating a list of favorite contacts on your iPhone can help you quickly access and communicate with the people who matter most to you. This list allows you to call, text, or email your favorite contacts from one convenient location. Follow these steps to create and customize your own favorites list: 1. **Open the Phone app.** The Phone app is typically found on your home screen or within the Utilities folder. It provides various communication tools such as calling, messaging, and video calls. 2. **Tap on 'Contacts' at the bottom of the screen.** This will open your complete list of saved contacts. Here, you can manage, edit, and delete contact information. 3. **Locate a contact you wish to add to your favorites list.** You can browse through your contacts alphabetically or use the search bar at the top of the screen to find a specific person. 4. **Tap on the contact's name.** This will open their contact details, including phone numbers, emails, addresses, and any notes associated with that contact. 5. **Scroll down and tap 'Add to Existing Group...'** If this option does not appear, first ensure that you have created a group by tapping 'Groups' in the upper left corner, then selecting 'Edit Groups,' and adding a new group called 'Favorites.' 6. **Select 'Favorites' from the list of groups.** Tapping on 'Favorites' will reveal two options: 'Create New Contact' (for adding a new contact directly to the Favorites group) and 'Add to Favorites' (to include an existing contact). 7. **Choose 'Add to Favorites'.** By doing so, you are designating this particular contact as a favorite, making them easily accessible via the Favorites tab. 8. **Decide which number or email address should be set as the default.** For example, if there are multiple phone numbers listed under the same contact, select the preferred number to streamline future communications. 9. **Confirm your selection by tapping 'Done' in the upper right corner.** Your chosen contact is now part of your favorites list! Repeat the process for additional individuals you would like to include. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - To remove a contact from your favorites list, navigate back to their contact details, scroll down, and deselect the 'Favorites' checkbox. Confirm removal by pressing 'Done.' - Access your favorites list quickly by opening the Phone app and tapping on the 'Favorites' icon at the bottom of the screen. All favorited contacts will appear here for easy communication. - Customize the sort order of your favorites list by going to 'Settings > Phone > Sort Order' and choosing between 'First, Last' or 'Last, First.' - Use Siri voice commands to initiate conversations with your favorite contacts more efficiently. Example phrases include "Call John," "Text Sarah," or "FaceTime Mom." - Regularly update your favorites list to keep up with changes in personal relationships and contact information. Title: How to Convert Feet to Yards Converting feet to yards is a simple process that involves understanding the relationship between these two units of measurement. Both feet and yards are used to measure length or distance. One yard is equivalent to three feet. This conversion factor makes it easy to convert measurements from one unit to another. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to do this: **Step 1: Understand the Relationship Between Feet and Yards** As mentioned earlier, one yard equals three feet. This means if you have one yard, you can also say you have three feet. The symbol for feet is 'ft', while the symbol for yards is 'yd'. So, when converting feet to yards, remember you divide the number of feet by three (3). Key Tip: Remembering that there are 3 feet in a yard will help make conversions easier. It might be helpful to memorize this fact or write it down as a reminder. **Step 2: Identify the Number of Feet You Want to Convert** To begin the conversion process, first identify the quantity of feet you wish to convert into yards. For example, let's assume we want to convert 9 feet into yards. **Step 3: Apply the Conversion Factor** Now, take the identified number of feet (in our case, 9) and divide it by the conversion factor (which is 3), since one yard equals three feet. Using basic arithmetic, we perform the division as follows: 9 ft 3 = 3 yd So, 9 feet is equal to 3 yards. Guideline: Make sure to carry out the calculation correctly, especially paying attention to whether your calculator is set to display results in whole numbers or decimals. If necessary, round off your answer according to significant figures rules. **Step 4: Confirm Your Answer** After performing the calculation, double-check your work to ensure accuracy. Use online tools, scientific calculators, or physical measuring instruments to confirm your result. In our example, using any reliable tool should give you an answer consistent with our calculated value of 3 yards. Key Tips: Be aware of common pitfalls such as confusing multiplication with division, forgetting to include the conversion factor, or making errors during calculations. Always review your work carefully before finalizing. **Step 5: Practice Regularly** Practice different problems involving foot-to-yard conversions until you feel comfortable doing them without assistance. Over time, this task will become second nature. Try converting various lengths like 12 feet, 18 feet, 27 feet, etc., into yards. By following these steps, you now know how to convert feet to yards accurately and confidently. Happy measuring! You can just skip it and go to the next one. An orchestra is a group of musicians playing instruments together. They make music. A large orchestra is sometimes called a "symphony orchestra" and a small orchestra is called a "chamber orchestra". A symphony orchestra may have about 100 players, while a chamber orchestra may have 30 or 40 players. The number of players will depend on what music they are playing and the size of the place where they are playing. The word "orchestra" originally meant the semi-circular space in front of a stage in a Greek theatre which is where the singers and instruments used to play. Gradually the word came to mean the musicians themselves. Is she your cousin or is she your friend? Adam where are you going with the cookies? Had a bad last year in school [laughs]. Title: How to Create a Poll on Snapchat Snapchat is a popular multimedia messaging app that allows users to share photos, videos, and messages with friends and followers. One fun feature of Snapchat is the ability to create polls within snaps, enabling you to gather opinions or engage your audience more interactively. This tutorial will guide you through creating a poll on Snapchat using an iPhone as an example device. However, the steps should be similar across different platforms (Android, etc.). **Step 1:** Open Snapchat App Find the yellow-colored ghost icon labeled "Snapchat" on your home screen or app drawer, then tap it to open. If this is your first time launching the app after installation, sign up or log in using your credentials. **Step 2:** Access Camera Screen Once logged in, swipe right from the camera viewfinder to access the chat window. Then swipe left twice rapidly to return to the camera interface. The camera screen is where you can capture pictures or record videos for adding poll stickers. **Step 3:** Capture Photo or Video To take a picture, simply press the circular button at the bottom center. To record a video, hold down the same button until satisfied; release when finished recording. After capturing media, proceed to add the poll sticker. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - Try taking shots under good lighting conditions for better image quality. - Add filters before capturing media by swiping left or right over the preview screen. **Step 4:** Navigate Sticker Drawer Tap the square smiling face icon located on the top right corner above the 'Send' button to reveal various sticker categories including text, emojis, Bitmoji, GIFs, Time, Local Weather, and Geofilters among others. Scroll horizontally to find the 'Poll' option usually positioned towards the end of these categories. **Step 5:** Select Poll Sticker Choose the 'Poll' sticker. It looks like two white boxes - one checked and another unchecked - against a purple background. Once selected, drag and drop it onto your snap. You may resize or rotate the sticker according to preference. **Step 6:** Customize Your Question Double-tap the added poll sticker to input your question and answer options. Type your question in the header field provided, followed by Answer Option A and Answer Option B below. Make sure they are clear, concise, and engaging enough to prompt responses. **Step 7:** Send Your Snap With Poll After customizing your question and answers, click the blue arrow button at the bottom right corner to send your snap privately to individual friends or via Group Chat. Alternatively, publish it publicly to My Story or Our Story if intended for broader audiences. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - Keep questions simple yet interesting to encourage participation. - Avoid leading questions that might sway voters towards specific choices. - Be mindful about sensitive topics which could potentially offend viewers. By following these steps carefully, you'll have successfully created and shared a poll on Snapchat! Now sit back and watch results roll in. Remember, keeping content lighthearted, respectful, and entertaining generally leads to higher engagement rates. Happy snapping! You're moving his trunk to put his hat on his head? This section has information about how to use pronouns correctly. Contributors:Chris Berry, Allen Brizee Last Edited: 2010-04-17 05:56:40 Pronoun Case is really a very simple matter. There are three cases. - Subjective case: pronouns used as subject. - Objective case: pronouns used as objects of verbs or prepositions. - Possessive case: pronouns which express ownership. |Pronouns as Subjects||Pronouns as Objects||Pronouns that show Possession| |he, she, it||him, her, it||his, her (hers), it (its)| The pronouns This, That, These, Those, and Which do not change form. Some problems of case: 1. In compound structures, where there are two pronouns or a noun and a pronoun, drop the other noun for a moment. Then you can see which case you want. Not: Bob and me travel a good deal. (Would you say, "me travel"?) Not: He gave the flowers to Jane and I. (Would you say, "he gave the flowers to I"?) Not: Us men like the coach. (Would you say, "us like the coach"?) 2. In comparisons. Comparisons usually follow than or as: He is taller than I (am tall). This helps you as much as (it helps) me. She is as noisy as I (am). Comparisons are really shorthand sentences which usually omit words, such as those in the parentheses in the sentences above. If you complete the comparison in your head, you can choose the correct case for the pronoun. Not: He is taller than me. (Would you say, "than me am tall"?) 3. In formal and semiformal writing: Use the subjective form after a form of the verb to be. Formal: It is I. Informal: It is me. Use whom in the objective case. Formal: To whom am I talking? Informal: Who am I talking to? You know do you want me to not influence her? You like the apple juice and the spoon. Let's see you think Mommy can stack these up? Put your hand right down like that. I bet there's a lot of fun things in this book to do. Behaving ethically is an important aspect of traveling anywhere in the world, including Africa. By following ethical guidelines, you can help preserve local cultures, protect the environment, and ensure that your travels benefit the communities you visit. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to behave ethically in Africa: **Step 1: Respect Local Cultures and Customs** Africa is a continent with diverse cultures, languages, and traditions. It's essential to research and understand the specific customs and etiquette of the region you plan to visit. This includes learning basic phrases in the local language, dressing appropriately, and being mindful of cultural norms around greetings, gift-giving, and social interactions. *Key Tip:* Always ask permission before taking photographs of people or their properties. Some communities may consider this intrusive or disrespectful. **Step 2: Support Local Businesses and Economy** One of the best ways to support local communities is by spending your money at locally owned businesses. This could include staying at small guesthouses or homestays instead of international hotel chains, eating at family-run restaurants, and purchasing handmade crafts from local artisans. *Key Guideline:* Avoid buying items made from endangered species or exploitative labor practices. Instead, look for fair trade certified products. **Step 3: Protect the Environment** Many African countries have fragile ecosystems that are highly susceptible to damage from tourism. To minimize your impact, stick to marked trails when hiking, don't litter, and be cautious about where you swim or bathe (to avoid contaminating water sources). *Key Tip:* Use reef-safe sunscreen if swimming in marine environments to protect coral reefs. **Step 4: Observe Wildlife Responsibly** Africa is home to some of the most incredible wildlife on Earth. When observing animals, maintain a safe distance and never feed them. Also, refrain from supporting activities that harm or stress animals, such as riding elephants or posing with sedated predators. *Key Guideline:* Book game drives with reputable companies that adhere to strict wildlife viewing regulations. **Step 5: Practice Safe Sex** Unfortunately, HIV/AIDS remains a significant health issue in many parts of Africa. Therefore, practicing safe sex is crucial. Carry condoms with you and make sure they are properly stored to prevent damage from heat or moisture. *Key Tip:* If you need medical attention while abroad, seek out clinics that follow universal precautions and use sterilized equipment. **Step 6: Respect Religious Sites and Practices** Religion plays a vital role in many African societies. If visiting religious sites, dress modestly and behaved respectfully. Learn about any rules or restrictions beforehand to avoid offending locals. *Key Guideline:* Drones might be considered offensive near sacred sites; check local laws and customs before using one. **Step 7: Stay Informed About Political Situations** Political climates can change rapidly in various African nations. Before travel, familiarize yourself with current events and government advisories related to safety and security. *Key Tip:* Register with your embassy so they can contact you during emergencies. By following these steps, you can contribute positively to the places you visit, fostering cross-cultural understanding and promoting sustainable tourism. How do you make a capital v? Goal: To successfully execute an arm bar, transition into an omoplata, and then finish with a triangle choke during a jiu-jitsu match. 1. Start in the guard position: Have your opponent in your guard (between your legs) while you are on your back. Your partner should have one hand controlling your opposite sleeve and the other gripping your belt or pants. This is the starting point for most guards in jiujitsu. A. Controlling their posture: Keep your partner's posture broken by using both hands to push on their chest or head. Maintaining this control will prevent them from posting their hands on the mat and escaping your guard. 2. Transitioning to the arm bar: With your partner's posture broken, free one leg and place it behind their head. Use your other foot to hook around their far leg trapping their leg between yours. Make sure that your shin is across their neck and your ankle is near their hip. Now grab their wrist with both hands. A. Ensuring proper grip: Hold their wrist tightly so they can't pull their arm out. You want to make sure that when you extend your hips, their elbow points directly up towards the ceiling. B. Hip extension: Drive your hips forward as if trying to touch your feet to your head. As you do this, lift your hips off the ground, which will hyperextend their elbow causing pain and forcing them to tap out. If they don't tap, keep applying pressure until they do. 3. Switching to the omoplata: After securing the initial arm bar attempt, release your hooks and bring both legs together. Swivel your body counterclockwise, keeping your same side leg trapped under their armpit. Your legs should now be forming a '4' shape around their torso. A. Securing the position: Place your top foot on the mat next to their hip, keeping your bottom leg over their shoulder. Grip their wrist again with both hands. B. Applying the submission: Push their wrist down towards the mat while pulling your legs towards your head. This movement rolls them onto their side creating tension in their shoulder joint. Continue rolling them further over until they submit or you run out of space. 4. Escaping the omoplata and setting up the triangle: Once your opponent taps or shows signs of relief, quickly swing your top leg over their head, landing perpendicular to their body. At the same time, thread your other leg through the hole created by your first leg, capturing their head and arm. A. Adjusting the triangle setup: Scoot your butt closer to their head, pinching your knees together to tighten the triangle. Bring your far arm around their head, locking your hands together to secure the choke. 5. Finishing the triangle: While maintaining control of their head and arm, lean backward, extending your spine. Apply downward pressure with your arms, squeezing your knees even tighter against their neck. Remember to breathe normally throughout the process; holding your breath makes it harder to maintain the position. A. Forcing the tapout: Eventually, your opponent will either submit due to lack of air or pass out. Release the hold immediately upon tapping or verbal confirmation of unconsciousness. Always prioritize safety above victory. [as DUP places tea set in the box] If we didn't have a zipper we couldn't get it open to get in to. Title: How to Get a Comprehensive Marine Survey Done A marine survey is an essential process when buying, selling, insuring, or maintaining a boat. It provides a thorough evaluation of the vessel's condition, safety standards, and seaworthiness. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the steps required to get a professional marine survey done with clarity, detail, and practicality. ### Step 1: Determine the Purpose of the Survey Before initiating the survey process, identify its purpose. Common reasons include pre-purchase inspection, insurance requirements, appraisal for financing, damage assessment, or general maintenance purposes. Knowing the objective ensures that you request the appropriate type and level of survey from a qualified marine surveyor. ### Step 2: Select a Qualified Marine Surveyor Search for reputable marine surveyors who hold certifications from recognized organizations such as the National Association of Marine Surveyors (NAMS), Society of Accredited Marine Surveyors (SAMS), or International Institute of Marine Surveying (IIMS). Request references, review their credentials, experience, and expertise related to your specific vessel type. Choose someone local if possible, but don't compromise quality over convenience. ### Step 3: Schedule the Appointment Contact your chosen marine surveyor and schedule an appointment at a mutually agreeable time and location. Provide necessary details about your boat, including make, model, year, size, and intended usage. Also, discuss any particular concerns or issues you want them to address during the survey. ### Step 4: Prepare Your Vessel for Inspection Prioritize cleanliness inside and outside the boat to facilitate easy accessibility and visual inspection. Remove personal belongings, clutter, or debris blocking areas needing examination. Make sure all systems are functioning correctly; this includes navigation lights, electrical components, engines, pumps, and plumbing fixtures. If possible, have recent service records available for review by the surveyor. ### Step 5: Attend the Survey (Optional) Although optional, attending the survey can be beneficial for gaining firsthand knowledge about your boat's condition and asking questions directly to the surveyor. However, keep in mind that your presence might affect the surveyor's objectivity, so maintain a respectful distance while they work. ### Step 6: Review the Survey Report After completing the inspection, the marine surveyor will provide a detailed written report outlining findings, observations, recommendations, and photographs. Familiarize yourself with industry terminology used in the document. Pay close attention to sections discussing deficiencies, repairs needed, estimated costs, and overall condition ratings. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Be prepared to pay reasonable fees for a high-quality marine survey, which typically range between $15-$30 per foot depending on the scope of services requested. * Understand that various types of surveys exist (e.g., condition and value survey, damage inspection, or specialized system assessments like electrical or mechanical surveys), each serving different objectives. * Remember that even new boats should undergo a pre-delivery inspection to ensure compliance with manufacturer specifications and regulatory requirements. * Don't hesitate to seek clarification from the marine surveyor regarding unclear aspects of the report or suggested actions. * Use the survey results as leverage when negotiating prices during boat purchases or justifying repair costs in insurance claims. Mark III's first album, "Burn", released in 1974, was successful and seemed to point a positive picture for the band's future without Gillan or Glover. However, there are also times when we use the past tense to express something else. a). To express a wish A wish is not something that happened in the past, but we need to use the past tense. I wish I had a car. (I do not have a car.) He wishes he won the lottery. In the above sentences, the past tense has nothing to do with the past time. b). In the sentence pattern "It's time " When "It's time" followed by a clause, the past tense is used in the clause. It's time we left. It's time we took design seriously. It's time you sold your old car. Here we are not talking about what happened in the past. If I say "It's time you sold your old car." to you, you still have your car, and you are driving it. c). To show politeness Sometimes we use the past tense because we want to be polite. The past tense is more polite than the present tense. I wonder if you can come to dinner tonight. I wondered if you could come to dinner tonight. I was wondering if you could come to dinner tonight. If you are inviting a friend, you use the first sentence. If you are inviting your boss, you probably will use the second or the third sentence. The third, the past progressive tense, is even more polite than the second. d) In the "if" clause We use the past tense in the "if" clause, if something is not a fact, or it's unlikely to happen. If it rained tomorrow, I would not come. (I think it will rain tomorrow.) If I got a raise, I would throw a party. (I don't believe I will get a raise.) When we use the simple present tense, it means that we believe it will happen. If it rains tomorrow, I will not come. (I don't think it will rain tomorrow.) If I get a raise, I will throw a party. (I think I will get a raise.) Generally speaking, the past tense is used to express the past time, but there are special uses of the past tense. We need to pay attention to these special uses in learning English. - Ron Lee I'm going to throw Mary in the water. And the clerk said twelve dollars and thirty-five cents. Mean and Variance: Understanding Your Data Imagine you are collecting information about your classmates, like their ages or heights. To make sense of all these numbers, we can calculate some special values that give us insight into our data. Two important ones are the mean (or average) and the variance. Let me explain! Mean: Imagine you want to know the typical age of your classmates. You could add up everyone's ages and divide by the total number of kids in your class. That would give you the mean age. In math, we write this formula as: mean = (age_1 + age_2 + ... + age\_n) / number of students Variance: Now let's think about how different each person's age is from the mean. Some may be close, while others far off. This tells us how spread out the ages are. We square the differences (to ensure they're always positive), find the average, and call it the variance: variance = ((difference_1)^2 + (difference_2)^2 + ... + (difference\_n)^2) / (number of students - 1) Here, difference means how much every student's age differs from the mean age calculated earlier. Let's try with an example: Say there are five friends in a classroom with the following ages: 8, 9, 7, 10, and 9 years old. 1. Find the mean: Add up all the ages: 8+9+7+10+9=43. Divide by the number of students: 43/5 = 8.6 years old. 2. Calculate variances: Subtract the mean from each age (e.g., 8.6 - 8 = 0.6), square those results (e.g., 0.6^2 = 0.36), and finally take the average of them all: (0.36 + 0.01 + 0.36 + 0.09 + 0.01)/(5-1). So the variance is approximately 0.15. In short, calculating the mean helps us understand the middle point of our dataset, whereas finding the variance shows how diverse or similar the collected data points are. These two concepts lay the foundation for further statistical analysis and understanding the world around us through numbers! Title: How to Check Your Trailer Is Safe Before Towing Introduction: Towing a trailer can be a daunting task if you're not properly prepared. It requires careful attention to detail and adherence to safety protocols. This tutorial will guide you through the necessary steps to ensure your trailer is safe before towing, reducing potential risks and ensuring a smooth journey. Step 1: Inspect the Hitch System The hitch system is crucial for connecting your vehicle to the trailer. Begin by checking the condition of the hitch ball, making sure it's free from cracks or corrosion. If it's removable, confirm that it's tightly secured. Next, inspect the hitch receiver - it should not have any deformities or signs of wear. Finally, examine the hitch coupler, ensuring it matches your trailer's size and is correctly latched onto the hitch ball. Key Tip: Always use a hitch rated for the weight of your loaded trailer to prevent accidents due to overloading. Step 2: Examine the Brake System (if applicable) If your trailer has brakes, they must be functioning correctly. Look at the brake lines for leaks, damage, or excessive wear. Also, test the brake controller in your tow vehicle to make sure it activates the trailer brakes when applied. Guideline: Most states require trailers over a certain weight to have their own braking system. Be aware of local regulations regarding this matter. Step 3: Check the Lighting System Proper lighting is essential for road safety during night-time towing. Verify that all lights are working correctly, including tail lights, brake lights, turn signals, and reverse lights. Replace any burnt-out bulbs immediately. Remember, malfunctioning lights can lead to dangerous situations and traffic violations. Step 4: Assess the Wheels and Tires Examine the tires thoroughly. They need to be inflated according to the manufacturer's recommendations (usually found on the sidewall). Check for adequate tread depth; less than 2/32 inches could cause blowouts or poor traction. Additionally, look out for uneven tire wear which might indicate alignment issues. Don't forget about the spare! Key Tip: Consider factors like load distribution and weather conditions while selecting appropriate tires for your trailer. Step 5: Review Load Distribution and Securement Ensure even weight distribution across the axles of your fully loaded trailer. An unbalanced load may result in unstable handling or damage to the trailer structure. Once balanced, secure your cargo using straps, ropes, or chains designed specifically for towing applications. Never rely solely on friction between items to keep them steady. Step 6: Test the Safety Chains Safety chains serve as backup support if something were to happen with the primary hitch connection. Cross the chains under the tongue of the trailer creating an X shape, then attach them to the frame of your tow vehicle. This setup prevents the tongue from dropping down if disconnected and reduces sway. Step 7: Confirm Visibility and Mirrors Before setting off, double-check your visibility. Make sure nothing obstructs your view out of the rear window. Install extended mirrors if needed, so you can clearly see around and behind the trailer while driving. Conclusion: By following these seven steps meticulously, you significantly enhance the safety of your trailer towing experience. Regular inspections and maintenance checks help maintain optimal performance and reduce risks associated with trailer transportation. Stay vigilant, drive responsibly, and enjoy your journey! Arrange a deck of cards in alternating colors, black and red. Now cut the deck so that the bottom card of one pile is black and the other is red. Riffle-shuffle the two piles together again. Now remove cards from the top of the pack in pairs. How many of these pairs should we expect to contain cards of differing colors? Surprisingly, all of them will. During the shuffle, suppose a black card falls first. It must be followed by either the next card in its own pile, which is red, or the first card from the other pile, which is also red. Either way, this first pair will contain one black card and one red card, and by the same principle so will each of the other 25 pairs produced by the shuffle. This effect was first identified by mathematician Norman Gilbreath in 1958. Related: Arrange the deck in a repeating cycle of suits, such as spade-heart-club-diamond, spade-heart-club-diamond, etc. Ranks don't matter. Now deal about half of this deck onto the table and riffle-shuffle the two halves back together. If you draw cards from the top in groups of four, you'll find that each quartet contains one card of each suit. See So Much for Entropy. Okay so I just have a few questions for you. It occurs in all parts of the world. It is fibrous, heat resistant and non flammable. These properties made it very attractive as a component in building materials and other products such as insulation and automobile brake pads. People have used asbestos throughout history. Ancient Egyptian people used asbestos. Asbestos is extremely dangerous for human health, as it can cause lung diseases such as asbestosis, emphysema and mesothelioma if the fibres get loose in the air. It can also harm animals such as livestock or pets. You pull 'em apart with the sheep? Women aren't sailors honey they almost never sailors. Do you want to read it to me? A run is a thin line or tear in the fabric that can quickly turn into a large hole, making your hosiery unwearable. Every time the child reads that word-and can read that word-he'll feel successful. And that's when you praise the heck out of him. Here's how it would look ME: This is a book about a mouse named Snappy. Look at that name, "Snappy." See the big S at the front? It's like a snake, isn't it? How many letters does Snappy's name have-let's count them. Six! What else does Snappy's name have? KID: Two of these letters. (pointing). ME: That's right! Snappy's name has two ps! And do you know this letter? (Pointing to the y.) OK, so now the child will recognize that if there's a word with two ps and a capital S and a y, it's likely to be "Snappy." You've shifted his focus from all of the grey text in the book, to looking for just one little word. And you've chosen a word that will come up a lot in the book, so there will be lots of successes. And now as you're reading out loud, pause whenever the word "Snappy" is in the text. And you know who's going to read that word? (Right!) The kid. The first couple of times you'll pause and point to the word, and maybe point out the capital S and the ps with your finger. And then look pointedly at the child, as if waiting for him to say ME: Good one! That's right! There's that word: Snappy! Nice job. I wonder if it's in here again And sure enough, the second time you pause, he'll notice the capital S and call out, "Snappy!" And you'll both be delighted. And the next time it will happen faster, and the next time you won't even have to pause at all. It will become seamless, like this: ME: One day when (child: Snappy!) was in his bedroom, his mother called to him. (child: Snappy!) she called. Oh, (child: Snappy!). Come down and eat your dinner! And then later in the week, the child will see the word in some other context, in another book or in an ad (Snapple) and he'll be so proud that he knows that word. Is he sounding out the word using phonics? No, he's memorizing it by its shape and a couple of cues. And memorizing is an important part of learning to read, especially in the beginning. But more than that, your child has taken ownership of a word. He's taken ownership of a book with "his" word in it. He has learned that he can read something, and he's been successful. Title: How to Activate an End Portal in Minecraft ## Step 1: Gathering Essential Materials Before you can activate an End Portal, you need to collect some essential items. These include Eyes of Ender and Diamond Pickaxe. **Eyes of Ender:** You'll require 12 Eyes of Ender to trigger the activation process. To craft one Eye of Ender, combine Blaze Powder (obtained by defeating Blazes found in Nether Fortresses) with an Ender Pearl (acquired from slaying Endermen). **Diamond Pickaxe:** This is necessary because only diamond or netherite pickaxes can break through bedrock surrounding the portal. Key Tip: Always ensure your inventory has enough space for these materials. ## Step 2: Locating Strongholds Stronghold(s) house the End Portals within their labyrinthine structures underground. They are located using Eye of Enders. Throw an Eye into the air; it will float towards the nearest stronghold. Follow its trajectory until it falls into a block, then start digging at that spot. Guideline: Keep throwing multiple eyes till they converge on a single location indicating the closest stronghold entrance. ## Step 3: Exploring the Stronghold Navigate through the maze-like corridors of the stronghold carefully while looking out for traps like pressure plates connected to TNT blocks. Remember, there could be more than one stronghold but typically, players look for the one with the activated end portal. Key Tips: - Bring plenty of torches to light up dark areas. - Equip yourself with food, weapons, armor, and potions as protection against hostile mobs. ## Step 4: Identifying the End Portal Room The room containing the End Portal consists of a circular arrangement of frames made of iron bars filled with eye patterns. If any frame lacks an eye pattern, proceed to the next stage. ## Step 5: Placing the Eyes of Ender Place an Eye of Ender inside every empty eye socket around the perimeter of the End Portal structure. As soon as all slots have been filled, the portal activates revealing the glowing purple surface ready for travel. Key Points: - Each eye slot requires exactly one Eye of Ender. - Once activated, do not remove the eyes otherwise, the portal deactivates. ## Step 6: Entering the End Portal Jump onto the activated end portal blocks which sink slightly under your weight confirming readiness for entry. Leap onto another block within the circle to initiate teleportation to the End dimension. Caution: Only jump when certain about the stability of the platform. Falling between gaps may cause damage or death. By following this comprehensive guide meticulously, players should now successfully activate an End Portal in Minecraft. Good luck! Title: How to Make Perfect Broasted Potatoes at Home Introduction: Broasting is a cooking method that combines pressure frying and broiling to create crispy exterior while keeping the interior moist and tender. This technique results in delicious, restaurant-quality potatoes with minimal oil absorption. With this comprehensive guide, you will learn how to make perfect broasted potatoes at home using readily available equipment and ingredients. **Ingredients:** 1. 4 large russet potatoes (about 8 oz or 225 g each) 2. Canola or peanut oil for deep frying (around 3 cups or 700 mL) 3. 1 cup (240 mL) buttermilk 4. 1 cup (125 g) all-purpose flour 5. 1 tbsp (7 g) paprika 6. 1 tsp (3 g) garlic powder 7. 1 tsp (3 g) onion powder 8. 1 tsp (3 g) salt 9. 1/2 tsp (1 g) black pepper 10. 1/2 tsp (1 g) dried basil 1 Mom he's eating all of this. Title: How to Access Medium Drafts in Desktop Medium is a popular online publishing platform where you can read, write, and interact with other writers' content. If you are an author on Medium and want to access your drafts using the desktop version, this tutorial will guide you through the process. By following these steps, you'll be able to manage and edit your unpublished stories efficiently. **Step 1:** Open your preferred web browser and navigate to <https://medium.com>. This action takes you to the Medium homepage, which showcases various articles from different authors. *Key tip*: Make sure that your browser supports JavaScript since some features might not work without it. Also, ensure that you have stable internet connectivity during the entire process. **Step 2:** Log into your Medium account by clicking the 'Sign in' button located at the top right corner of the page. Enter your email address or username along with your password when prompted. Alternatively, if you have connected your Google or Apple ID with your Medium account, click one of those options instead. *Guideline*: Keeping yourself logged in allows faster access to your Medium profile and its functionalities. However, remember to log out after finishing your writing session if multiple people share the same computer or device. **Step 3**: Once logged in, hover over your profile picture (top-right corner) and select 'My Profile' from the dropdown menu. You'll now see your personalized Medium feed containing all your published articles as well as any pieces you've followed. *Key tip*: The number beside your profile picture indicates the total count of followers. Engaging with others' writings - such as clapping, commenting, or sharing - increases visibility and attracts more followers. **Step 4**: On your profile page, locate the 'Stories' tab below your banner image and click 'Drafts'. Here, you'll find every article currently saved but not yet published under your name. *Guidelines*: Organize your drafts regularly to prevent clutter and confusion. Delete unnecessary ones, archive completed works awaiting publication, and prioritize editing based on urgency or importance. **Step 5**: To open a specific draft, simply click on its title. It should load within the main window area, ready for further edits. Note that the editor opens in preview mode initially; switch to 'Write' view via the toggle button at the bottom center for full functionality. *Key tip*: Use Grammarly integration available directly inside the Medium editor for grammar suggestions and corrections while crafting your masterpiece! By completing these five steps, you successfully accessed your Medium drafts using the desktop version. Now go ahead, create compelling content, engage with fellow writers, and grow as a respected member of the Medium community! Title: How to Act Immediately After a First Kiss Introduction: Kissing is a significant milestone in any relationship, whether it's your first kiss with someone new or an established couple deepening their connection. The moments after sharing this intimate act can be just as important as the kiss itself. Learning how to act immediately afterward can help you navigate these situations with grace and confidence. This article will provide a comprehensive guide on what to do right after that magical moment. Step 1: Pause & Savor the Moment After the kiss, take a brief pause. Allow yourself a few seconds to absorb the experience and appreciate the emotion behind it. By doing so, you show respect for the other person and acknowledge the importance of the moment. It also gives both parties time to catch their breath and regain composure before moving forward. Key Tip: Maintain eye contact during this pause. Looking into each other's eyes can intensify the emotional bond created by the kiss and set the stage for further intimacy. Step 2: Gauge Their Reaction Observe the other person's reaction carefully. Are they smiling? Leaning in for another kiss? Or does it seem like they want some space? Interpreting their body language correctly is crucial in determining your next move. Guideline: Remember, everyone reacts differently to being kissed. Some people might appear shy or hesitant even if they enjoyed the kiss. Respect their feelings and give them room to process the situation. Step 3: Verbally Affirm Your Feelings If the timing feels right, verbally express your positive feelings about the kiss. You could say something simple yet sincere such as, "I really liked that," or "That was nice." Such statements reinforce the connection between you two and make the other person feel valued. Key Tip: Authenticity matters more than grandeur here. Speak from the heart rather than trying to find the perfect words. Honesty builds trust which is vital in any romantic relationship. Step 4: Engage in Light Post-Kiss Conversation Initiate a casual conversation following the kiss. Ask about their day, comment on shared interests, or bring up a funny incident - anything that keeps the mood light and friendly. This conversation helps diffuse potential tension and eases back into regular interaction. Guideline: Keep the topic neutral and avoid discussing the kiss directly unless prompted by your partner. Too much focus on the act itself may come off as intense or pressuring. Step 5: Respect Boundaries Pay attention to non-verbal cues throughout your interaction post-kiss. If your partner seems distant, uncomfortable, or eager to end the encounter, respect their boundaries. Everyone moves at different paces; pushing too hard could damage the budding relationship. Key Tip: Always obtain clear consent before making any advancements. Consent isn't just about sexual activities but also applies to physical touch and proximity. Conclusion: Acting appropriately after a first kiss involves mindfulness, empathy, and effective communication. Following these steps ensures you handle the situation delicately while strengthening the bond between you and your partner. Most importantly, remember to enjoy the moment and cherish the memories created! Show them your beautiful jewelry box you made. No put it down here 'cause there's not enough room. Title: How to Draw Manga Anime Eyes Drawing manga and anime characters is a popular art form that requires practice and patience. One of the most distinctive features of these styles are their expressive and unique eyes. This tutorial will guide you through the process of drawing manga anime eyes in 13 steps with clear explanations, key tips, and guidelines. Let's get started! ### Step 1: Understand the Basics Manga and anime eye shapes can vary greatly depending on the character's age, gender, emotions, or even species (e.g., human, alien, etc.). However, they generally consist of several basic components: upper eyelid, lower eyelid, iris, pupil, highlight(s), and reflections. Familiarize yourself with these parts before proceeding. ### Step 2: Choose an Eye Shape Decide on the shape of your character's eyes based on its personality and appearance. Common shapes include almond-shaped, round, droopy, wide-eyed, cat-like, or slanted. For this example, we'll use a standard almond-shape. ### Step 3: Sketch the Outer Shapes Lightly sketch two oval shapes for the upper lids and two shorter curves for the lower lids. Make sure the distance between both eyes fits the width of one eye for proper proportions. ### Step 4: Connect Upper and Lower Lids Most of the numbers that people use in everyday life are rational. These include fractions, integers and numbers with finite decimal digits. In general, a number that can be written as a fraction while it is in its own form is rational. This guide will provide you with in-depth steps and valuable insights to help you attract an older woman while being respectful, mature, and considerate. **Step 1: Understand the Differences Between Age Groups** Older women have different life experiences, perspectives, and needs compared to teenagers. Be aware of these differences and show genuine interest in understanding her background, values, and aspirations. This will demonstrate emotional intelligence and maturity, making you more attractive to her. *Key tip:* Avoid stereotypes about older women; instead, treat them as individuals with unique stories and personalities. **Step 2: Improve Yourself** Invest time in self-improvement by focusing on your physical health, mental wellbeing, social skills, and intellectual growth. Engage in regular exercise, maintain good hygiene, expand your knowledge through reading or taking courses, and learn effective communication strategies. These efforts will make you more confident and appealing to older women. *Guideline*: Aim for overall improvement rather than quick fixes. Consistency is crucial for building lasting appeal and character. **Step 3: Show Respect and Maturity** Treat all women, including those who are older, with the utmost respect. Listen attentively during conversations, ask thoughtful questions, and avoid engaging in immature behavior. Demonstrating maturity shows that you're capable of handling serious relationships and responsibilities. *Key tip:* Always seek consent before pursuing any romantic relationship. Remember that age does not equate to automatic interest or approval. **Step 4: Develop Common Interests** Discover shared hobbies, interests, or causes that bridge the age gap between you and the older woman. By doing so, you create opportunities for meaningful connections based on mutual passions and goals. *Guideline:* Focus on activities where both parties can actively participate and contribute. Shared learning experiences, such as attending workshops or classes together, also foster strong bonds. **Step 5: Communicate Openly and Honestly** Be open and honest about your intentions from the beginning. Clear communication prevents misunderstandings and fosters trust. However, avoid discussing age-related issues excessively, which may come across as obsessive or insincere. *Key tip:* Use appropriate language and tone to convey your thoughts without causing offense or discomfort. **Step 6: Value Her Independence** Respect the older woman's independence and individuality. Encourage her autonomy and avoid attempting to control or change her. Recognize that she has her own life, career, friends, and family, just like you do. *Guideline:* Balance spending quality time together with giving each other space to pursue separate interests. **Step 7: Handle Criticism Gracefully** As you navigate dating an older woman, expect constructive criticism and advice. Embrace this feedback graciously and use it as an opportunity for growth. Displaying humility and willingness to learn strengthens your bond and demonstrates maturity. *Key tip:* Approach every situation with curiosity and eagerness to understand her perspective. **Step 8: Manage Expectations Realistically** Recognize potential challenges due to societal norms, familial expectations, or generational gaps. Prepare yourself mentally and emotionally for obstacles along the way. Stay patient, persistent, and positive throughout the process. *Guideline:* Seek guidance from trusted sources if needed but ultimately rely on your judgment and instincts. Title: How to Beat 100 Floors: A Comprehensive Walkthrough 1. Understand the Game Basics * 100 Floors is a mobile puzzle game where you need to solve puzzles to advance to the next floor. The goal is to reach the 100th floor by solving all one hundred puzzles. * Each level has a unique puzzle that requires creative thinking, problem-solving skills, and keen observation. * There are no time limits or penalties for wrong attempts; this allows players to experiment with different solutions without pressure. Key Tips: * Observe your surroundings closely. Many puzzles require you to interact with objects in the room. * Experiment with various gestures (tapping, swiping, dragging) as they can trigger hidden mechanisms. * Take note of patterns, colors, numbers, and shapes - these elements often play a crucial role in solving puzzles. 2. Prepare Necessary Tools * Although most puzzles can be solved using basic touch interactions, some levels may require additional tools like a flashlight or other virtual items found within the game itself. * Be aware of when new tools become available and learn how they function so you'll know when to use them effectively. 3. Solve Puzzle Floor 1 - Elevator Buttons * Tap the '1' button first, then tap the '2' button. This opens the elevator door. * Tap and hold the 'up' arrow until the elevator reaches the second floor. Key Guidelines: * Learn early on that tapping and holding certain buttons will have different outcomes than just single taps. 4. Solve Puzzle Floor 2 - Light Switch * Swipe right across the screen to turn on the light switch. Key Tip: * Familiarize yourself with gesture controls from an early stage. 5. Solve Puzzle Floor 3 - Cracked Wall * Press down firmly on the cracked wall section until it breaks away revealing a keyhole. * Use the key from your inventory (accessed via the icon at bottom left corner) to unlock the door. 6. Solve Puzzle Floor 4 - Painting Pattern * Notice the pattern on the painting - three red stripes followed by two blue ones. * Mimic this pattern by tapping the corresponding color circles outside the elevator. 7. Solve Puzzle Floor 5 - Weight Scale * Place the heavy object (the block with a star) on the scale to balance it out. 8. Solve Puzzle Floor 6 - Coded Keypad * Input the sequence shown on the keypad above the elevator: 9-1-5-2. Key Tips: * Remember sequences, codes, and number combinations as they might reappear later. * Pay attention to details around the elevators as well since they often provide clues. 9. Continue Solving Floors... * Follow similar steps for subsequent floors, always observing your environment carefully and trying various interactive gestures. * Some solutions involve combining multiple actions or utilizing previously acquired tools. Remember, perseverance is vital! Don't get discouraged if you don't solve a particular floor immediately. Keep trying different approaches, and remember that trial and error is part of the process. Good luck beating those 100 floors! Am I sitting on the microphone cord? And what are you going to see on top of the refrigerator? The caterpillar that turns into the butterfly. Title: How to Tie a Sarong Difficulty: Easy Step 1: Choose your sarong Select a sarung that is large enough to wrap around your body. A typical sarong measures about 45 inches wide by 72 inches long. Sarongs come in various patterns and materials such as cotton, silk, or synthetic fabric. Pick one that suits your personal style and comfort. Key Tip: Opt for a lightweight, breathable material like cotton for maximum comfort, especially if you plan to wear the sarong in warm weather. Step 2: Lay out the sarong Lay the sarong flat on a solid surface with the printed side facing down. Make sure the edges are even and there are no twists or folds in the fabric. This will ensure a smooth finish when wrapping the sarong around your body. Guideline: Keeping the sarong untangled before starting makes the process easier and less frustrating. Step 3: Fold the sarong in half (optional) For a more manageable size, fold the sarong in half lengthwise so that its width becomes half while keeping its length unchanged. This step can be skipped if you prefer a fuller look or have a smaller sarong. Key Tip: If you choose to fold the sarong, make sure the folded edge aligns neatly along the entire length. Step 4: Drape the sarong over your shoulder Hold the sarong at both ends with equal lengths hanging down from your hands. Place one end over your left shoulder, letting the rest of the fabric drape down your back. Bring the other end across your chest diagonally, holding it securely under your right arm. The fabric should now form an X shape on your torso. Guideline: Adjust the position of the sarong according to where you want the knot to sit - higher up for a cropped top effect or lower down for a dress-like appearance. Step 5: Create a twist Gather the two ends of the sarong together where they cross on your chest. Twist them together firmly several times until they resemble a thick rope. This creates a secure base for tying the knot and prevents the sarong from slipping off during wear. Key Tip: Maintain tension throughout the twisted section to keep the sarong snug against your body. Step 6: Form the final knot Bring the twisted fabric around your back and then forward again, forming a loop. Pass one end of the twisted fabric through this loop and pull tight to create a secure double knot. Position the knot either at the front or side depending on your preference. Guideline: Always check that the knot is tightly secured but still comfortable. Loosen or tighten the knot accordingly. Step 7: Adjust the fit and fabric Once the knot is tied, adjust the remaining fabric to achieve your desired look. Smooth out any wrinkles or creases for a polished finish. For extra coverage, gather excess fabric at the hips and tie another knot or add a belt. Key Tip: Play around with different ways of arranging the fabric - pleats, gathers, or cascading layers - to create unique styles tailored to your taste. With practice, you'll master the art of tying a sarong quickly and confidently, transforming it into a versatile wardrobe staple suitable for various occasions. Title: How to Care for an Overweight Cat Introduction: Obesity is a common health issue among domestic cats, often resulting from overfeeding, lack of exercise, or underlying medical conditions. This guide will provide you with comprehensive steps to help your overweight cat reach a healthy weight and maintain it. Remember that every cat is unique; therefore, consult with your veterinarian before making any significant changes to your cat's diet or exercise routine. Step 1: Consult Your Veterinarian Determine if your cat is indeed overweight by consulting your vet. They can assess your cat's body condition score (BCS) accurately, identify any potential health issues causing obesity, and recommend a safe weight loss plan tailored to your cat's needs. Key Tip: Keep track of your cat's current weight and BCS during regular check-ups to monitor progress effectively. Step 2: Assess Feeding Habits Examine your feeding practices, including portion sizes, meal frequency, and treats given throughout the day. Be aware that free-choice feeding, where food is available at all times, may contribute to excessive eating and obesity. Guideline: Ideally, feed your cat twice daily using measured portions according to their recommended caloric intake provided by your vet. Avoid giving table scraps or high-calorie treats. Step 3: Choose a Weight Management Diet Select a high-quality commercial diet specifically formulated for weight management, ideally one endorsed by your vet. These diets are designed to promote gradual weight loss while ensuring essential nutrients are still met. Key Tip: Gradually transition your cat onto the new diet over seven days to prevent digestive upset. Mix increasing amounts of the new food with decreasing quantities of the old until fully switched. Step 4: Measure Food Accurately Measure meals precisely using gram scales or measuring cups instead of guessing. Portion control is crucial when managing feline obesity. Guideline: Follow the feeding guideline suggested by the manufacturer based on your cat's ideal weight rather than their current weight. Adjust as needed under veterinary supervision. Step 5: Limit Treats and Snacks Offer low-calorie treats sparingly as rewards during training sessions or positive behavior reinforcement. Consider alternatives like green beans, carrots, or cooked lean meats, but always consult your vet first. Key Tip: Never exceed 10% of your cat's total daily calories from treats or snacks. Step 6: Encourage Physical Activity Engage your cat in playtime activities several times a day. Use interactive toys such as laser pointers, feather wands, or balls to stimulate hunting instincts and increase movement. Guideline: Aim for at least 30 minutes of active play per day, broken down into multiple short sessions. Make sure these interactions remain fun and enjoyable for both you and your cat. Step 7: Monitor Progress Regularly Weigh your cat weekly and record the results. If there isn't any noticeable weight loss after two weeks, reevaluate the feeding plan with your vet and make necessary adjustments. Key Tip: Expect slow yet consistent weight loss - aim for 0.5-1% of body weight per week. Rapid weight loss could lead to serious health problems. Conclusion: Caring for an overweight cat requires patience, consistency, and close collaboration with your veterinarian. By following this comprehensive guide, you'll be well equipped to help your furry friend regain their optimal health and happiness. No he drinks out of like a sippy cup. Tutorial on how to read and interpret pie charts. The pie chart below shows the percentages of blood types for a group of 200 people. a) How many people, in this group, have blood type AB? b) How many people, in this group, do not have blood type O? c) How many people, in this group, have blood types A or B? Solution to Example 1: a) 19% * 200 = 19 * 200 / 100 = 38 people b) (100% - 40%) * 200 = 60 * 200 / 100 = 120 people c) (16% + 25%) * 200 = 41 * 200 / 100 = 82 The pie chart below shows the percentages of types of transportation used by 800 students to come to school. a) How many students, in the school, come to school by bicycle? b) How many students do not walk to school? c) How many students come to school by bus or in a car? Solution to Example 2: a) 45% * 800 = 360 students b) (100% - 15%) * 800 = 680 students c) (30% + 10%) * 800 = 320 students The pie chart below shows the percentages of the world population in each continent. The present world population is about 7 billion. a) How many people live in Africa? b) How many people do not live in Asia than? c) How many more people live in North America than in South America? Solution to Example 3: a) 15% * 7 = 1.05 billion b) 7 - 60.4% * 7 = 2.772 billion c) (7.3% - 6%) * 7 = 91 million The total area of Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Europe and Australia is 134 million square kilometers. The pie chart below shows the percentages of each continent. a) What is the area of Asia? b) What is the area Europe? c) How much bigger is Africa than Europe? Solution to Example 4: a) 33.2% * 134 = 44.5 million square kilometers b) 7.5% * 134 = 10.5 million square kilometers c) (22.3% - 7.5%) * 134 = 19.8 million square kilometers More references on elementary statistics and probabilities. Stamen Grigorov (1878-1945), a Bulgarian student of medicine in Geneva, first examined the bacteria in Bulgarian yogurt. In 1905 he said that it contained a round and a rod-like lactic acid-producing bacteria. In 1907 the rod-like bacteria was called "Lactobacillus bulgaricus" (now "Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. bulgaricus"). The Russian Nobel laureate biologist Ilya Ilyich Mechnikov, from the Pasteur Institute in Paris, was influenced by Grigorov's work and made a hypothesis that eating yoghurt regularly was the reason why Bulgarian peasants lived for so long. Mechnikov believed that "Lactobacillus" was needed for good health, and worked to make yoghurt popular through Europe. Let's put it away [food in mouth].
